title: Coleus forskohlii (Coleus)

benefits.benefit_desc: Coleus improves cardiovascular health, meaning it supports the heart and blood vessels, promoting healthy blood flow and pressure. The main active constituents of Coleus forskohlii that contribute to this benefit are labdane diterpene, namely forskolin, and rosmarinic acid. The primary parts of the Coleus forskohlii plant that provide this benefit are the roots, where the highest concentration of forskolin is found. The main medicinal preparations of Coleus forskohlii that give this benefit include standardized extracts, capsules, and tinctures. This benefit of Coleus forskohlii helps relieve various health conditions, such as hypertension, angina, and congestive heart failure, by promoting healthy blood flow, reducing blood pressure, and improving overall cardiovascular function.

Coleus enhances bone strength, meaning it supports the durability and resilience of bones, making them less prone to fractures and breaks. The main active constituents of Coleus forskohlii responsible for this benefit are alkaloids, diterpenes, and triterpenes, with forskolin being the most notable. The primary parts of the Coleus forskohlii plant that give this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of forskolin. The main medicinal preparations of Coleus forskohlii that give this benefit are standardized extracts, tinctures, and capsules. This benefit of Coleus forskohlii helps relieve health conditions such as osteoporosis, bone density loss, and other age-related bone health issues.

Coleus boosts testosterone levels, meaning it helps increase the production of the primary male sex hormone, testosterone, in the body. The main active constituent of Coleus forskohlii responsible for this benefit is forskolin, a labdane diterpene that stimulates the production of cyclic AMP (cAMP), which in turn triggers various cellular responses related to testosterone production. The primary parts of the Coleus forskohlii plant that give this benefit are the roots, where the highest concentration of forskolin is found. The main medicinal preparations of Coleus forskohlii that give this benefit are standardized extracts, which are commonly available in the form of capsules or tablets. This benefit of Coleus forskohlii helps relieve various health conditions, including sexual dysfunction, infertility, and age-related decreases in testosterone levels.

Coleus eases asthma symptoms, meaning it can help alleviate the constriction of the airways and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe. The main active constituent of Coleus forskohlii responsible for this benefit is forskolin, a labdane diterpene that relaxes muscles in the airways. The primary parts of the Coleus forskohlii plant that provide this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of forskolin. The main medicinal preparations of Coleus forskohlii that give this benefit are extracts and tinctures, which can be taken orally or inhaled as a mist. This benefit of Coleus forskohlii helps relieve the symptoms of asthma, a chronic respiratory condition characterized by inflammation and narrowing of the airways, making it difficult to breathe.

Coleus forskohlii reducing inflammation means that it helps in alleviating redness, swelling, and pain caused by irritation or injury. The main active constituents of Coleus forskohlii that contribute to this benefit are the diterpenoids, particularly forskolin. The primary parts of the Coleus forskohlii plant that provide this benefit are the roots, where the highest concentration of forskolin is found. The main medicinal preparations of Coleus forskohlii that give this benefit are extracts in the form of liquid tinctures, capsules, or powdered root. This benefit of Coleus forskohlii helps relieve various health conditions such as arthritis, asthma, and allergies, where inflammation plays a significant role in their pathophysiology.

Coleus fights skin disorders, meaning it can help alleviate various skin conditions such as allergic contact dermatitis, eczema, and psoriasis. The main active constituent of Coleus forskohlii responsible for this benefit is forskolin, a labdane diterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The primary parts of the Coleus forskohlii plant that give this benefit are the roots, which contain the highest concentration of forskolin. The main medicinal preparations of Coleus forskohlii that give this benefit are extracts and tinctures, which can be applied topically or taken orally. This benefit of Coleus forskohlii helps relieve skin disorders by reducing inflammation, inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria, and promoting the healing of damaged skin.

Coleus forskohlii lowers high blood pressure, meaning it helps reduce the force of blood against the walls of your arteries, making it easier for the heart to pump blood. The main active constituents of Coleus forskohlii that contribute to this benefit are Forskolin and rosmarinic acid. The primary parts of the Coleus forskohlii plant that provide this benefit are the roots and leaves. The main medicinal preparations of Coleus forskohlii that give this benefit include oral supplements, such as capsules or tablets, and liquid extracts. This benefit of Coleus forskohlii helps relieve health conditions such as hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and other complications associated with high blood pressure.

Coleus prevents blood clots, meaning it helps to reduce the risk of harmful clots forming in the blood vessels and obstructing blood flow. The main active constituents of Coleus forskohlii that contribute to this benefit are labdane diterpenes, including the most notable one, forskolin. The primary parts of the Coleus forskohlii plant that possess this benefit are the roots, where the highest concentration of forskolin is found. The main medicinal preparations of Coleus forskohlii that provide this benefit are standardized extracts, typically containing 10-20% forskolin. This benefit of Coleus forskohlii helps relieve health conditions such as deep vein thrombosis, heart disease, and stroke, all of which can be caused or worsened by the formation of blood clots.

Coleus forskohlii supports healthy thyroid function, meaning it helps maintain the optimal performance of the thyroid gland, which is crucial for various bodily functions such as metabolism regulation and hormone production. The main active constituents of Coleus forskohlii responsible for this benefit are the diterpenes, with forskolin being the most notable. The primary parts of the Coleus forskohlii plant that provide this benefit are the roots, where the highest concentration of forskolin is found. The main medicinal preparations of Coleus forskohlii that give this benefit include standardized extracts, capsules, and powdered root. This benefit of Coleus forskohlii helps relieve health conditions such as hypothyroidism, thyroid dysfunction, and other thyroid-related disorders.

Coleus aids in weight loss, meaning it can help individuals achieve a healthy weight by reducing body fat and increasing lean body mass. The main active constituent of Coleus forskohlii responsible for this benefit is forskolin, a labdane diterpene that activates the enzyme adenylate cyclase, which increases levels of cyclic AMP (cAMP) in cells, leading to lipolysis or the breakdown of fat in fat cells. The primary parts of the Coleus forskohlii plant that contain this beneficial compound are the roots, which are typically harvested and dried for medicinal use. The most common medicinal preparations of Coleus forskohlii for weight loss are standardized extracts, which are available in capsule or tablet form and contain a consistent amount of forskolin per dose. This benefit of Coleus forskohlii is particularly helpful for individuals who are overweight or obese, as it can improve body composition and overall health by reducing body fat and increasing lean muscle mass.

intro: Coleus forskohlii, a member of the mint family, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries and has been found to have several medicinal benefits. One of its most well-known uses is for improving cardiovascular health, as it helps relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure. Additionally, Coleus forskohlii has been shown to increase testosterone levels and improve bone health, making it a potential supplement for those with osteoporosis. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of asthma and other respiratory conditions. Furthermore, early research suggests that Coleus forskohlii may have potential as a weight loss aid, as it appears to stimulate the breakdown of fat in the body. However, more research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal benefits and potential side effects.
